PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Douglas Voorsanger, counsel, specially appearing for Anita Thomas, Petitioner.

Mr. Voorsanger notes at the outset that he specially appearing for Mr. Menzel but that he has reviewed the facts of the case with Mr. Menzel and is prepared to go forward. The Court questions why there is a need to admit both the July 27, 2015 writing and the September 18, 2015 writing into probate as it would appear that both writings are holographic wills and that the latter will would supersede and invalidate the earlier will. Mr. Voorsanger notes that while the latter will, such as it is, appears to be indicative of the testator's dispositive intent, the earlier will, such as it is, appears to be indicative of the testator's intent to identify an executor. As such, he would proffer the July 27, 2015 writing as the holographic will and the September 18, 2015 writing as a holographic codicil to the earlier "will." Mr. Voorsanger submits a proposed Order to that effect and the Court takes the matter under submission.

Nonetheless, the Court sets hearing for status of closing the estate for September 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., indicating its confidence that the estate would be closed long before that date.

Judge: Hon. Peter J. Busch; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Jaime; Not reported.
